name: Sentinel APEX Defense Solutions Storefront
description: The platform's flagship defense-product storefront (Sigma rules, YARA rules, firewall scripts, IR playbooks) generated from live CVE intelligence, with a full Razorpay purchase-and-verify flow.

frontend.evidence: The live product-card storefront ('Sentinel APEX Defense Solutions' heading) lives at frontend/index.html:2864-2921 with category filters matching workers/src/handlers/defenseSeed.js's seed categories (firewall_script, sigma_rule, yara_rule, ids_signature, ir_playbook, threat_hunt_pack, python_scanner). Full purchase trace confirmed end-to-end: the Unlock button (frontend/index.html:3121-3123 or the modal's :3431) calls dsPurchase(), which opens an email-capture step and then calls initiatePayment() (frontend/index.html:3230-3329), which POSTs to /api/defense/purchase/{id} (:3266-3270) and, on Razorpay success, POSTs to /api/defense/verify/{id} (:3300-3309) — both routes resolve to this handler's functions exactly. Verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: Linked from the mobile nav drawer ('🛡 Defense Marketplace', frontend/index.html:1186) plus multiple in-page CTA/scroll links. One caveat: the whole storefront section carries inline style="display:none" and only becomes visible via a client-side cdbApplyGates() check (frontend/index.html:434-440) gated on localStorage having both cdb_token and cdb_email (or isOwner) — an anonymous, signed-out visitor does not see this section rendered until that client-side condition passes. Whether cdbApplyGates() runs automatically on page load for a logged-out visitor (revealing nothing, correctly) versus hiding an otherwise-crawlable section from search/first-time visitors was not fully traced in this pass.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/revenuePlanFieldFix.test.mjs imports handleGetSolution directly from workers/src/handlers/defenseMarketplace.js — confirmed by import-statement grep. The other 8 exports (including the purchase/verify pair the live Unlock flow depends on) have no dedicated import-based test found in this pass.

verification.evidence: Full click-trace performed via direct code read: workers/src/handlers/defenseMarketplace.js's 9 exports enumerated; frontend/index.html:2864-3431 read directly to confirm the Unlock-to-purchase-to-verify chain resolves to this handler at every step, not a different one.

notes: This is the platform's most convincingly end-to-end-verified marketplace capability found in this pass — real UI, real click trace, real matching backend, confirmed by direct code read rather than inference from route existence alone. A dynamic_browser pass (actually clicking through a test purchase) is the natural next step to upgrade this to a fully verified GA claim. handleGetFeatured has no confirmed frontend caller; handleGenerateSolutions is an admin-only generation trigger, not a customer path.

name: Marketplace Catalog & Checkout (Generic)
description: General-purpose marketplace catalog browsing, checkout, and purchase-verification flow used by the dedicated marketplace checkout page.

frontend.evidence: frontend/marketplace-checkout.html:182 (catalog/:id), :291 (checkout), :347 (verify) call this handler's routes directly, matching field-for-field. frontend/sentinel-apex-marketplace.html:879 also calls the catalog route for its category-filtered listing. No frontend caller found for handleMarketplaceDownload, handleMyMarketplacePurchases, or handleMarketplaceObservability. Verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: frontend/marketplace-checkout.html is linked from frontend/ai-governance-pdf.html:128, frontend/mssp-onboarding.html:140, and frontend/sentinel-apex-marketplace.html (product-card / 'Open Marketplace' links).

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.test.mjs imports handleMarketplaceCatalog/handleMarketplaceProduct/handleMarketplaceCheckout/handleMarketplaceVerify/handleMarketplaceDownload/handleMyMarketplacePurchases/MARKETPLACE_CATALOG directly — the most test-covered handler found across this whole domain.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.js exports enumerated; workers/src/index.js:5530-5556 route registrations read directly; frontend/marketplace-checkout.html cross-checked against each route.

notes: P3 reflects handleMyMarketplacePurchases and handleMarketplaceObservability having no confirmed frontend caller (a 'my purchases' history view a customer would likely expect but cannot currently reach), not a defect in the core catalog/checkout/verify path, which is real and well-tested.

name: Sentinel-Specific Purchase Flow
description: A purpose-built purchase/verify pair for Sentinel APEX product types that the generic marketplace checkout modal has no price entry for.

frontend.evidence: frontend/sentinel-apex-marketplace.html:663 (purchase) and :689 (verify) call these routes directly, with an explanatory code comment at :605-607 stating this purpose-built pair exists because the generic checkout modal has no price entry for these product types. Verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: frontend/sentinel-apex-marketplace.html is linked from frontend/ciso-hub.html (3 locations), frontend/index.html (4 locations), frontend/user-dashboard.html (6 locations), and frontend/marketplace-checkout.html — not an orphan.

notes: No actionable gap beyond missing tests. Widely linked, real, working code path.

name: Security Tools Marketplace
description: Instant-checkout purchase flow for individual security tools (distinct from defense solutions and the generic marketplace), used consistently across multiple pages.

frontend.evidence: All 3 routes are called consistently from frontend/tools.html:1432,1658,1682, frontend/index.html:6935,6952 (with a comment at :6918 describing 'instant Razorpay checkout wired to /api/tools/purchase + /api/tools/verify'), frontend/user-dashboard.html:4205,4224, and frontend/intel.html:2371,2390. Verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: frontend/tools.html and frontend/intel.html are each linked from at least 8 other pages including frontend/index.html — not orphans.

test_coverage.evidence: No test file imports workers/src/handlers/toolsMarketplace.js directly, despite being the most widely-consumed handler (4 distinct pages) found in this domain.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/toolsMarketplace.js read directly (3 exports); all 4 consuming frontend pages grepped for the 3 route paths and cross-checked.

notes: Real, consistently-implemented, multi-page purchase flow with a real gap: zero test coverage despite being the most widely used handler in this domain.

name: Sentinel APEX Marketplace Mega-Dispatcher (Subscriptions, Entitlements, ROI Calculator)
description: A single internal mega-handler covering marketplace purchase/subscription/entitlement/order-history/trial/ROI-calculator/plan-comparison actions. Previously also carried three dead, shadowed catalog/product/checkout routes (removed 2026-07-11); now known to resolve products from a catalog with zero ID overlap with the one customers actually browse (see notes).

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-11 (dead-code half only): workers/src/handlers/sentinelApexMarketplace.js's handleGetCatalog/handleGetProduct/handleCheckout — permanently unreachable since workers/src/index.js:5617-5624 registers exact-match routes for the same 3 paths earlier via workers/src/handlers/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.js (CAP-MKT-002, the real live implementation, confirmed by frontend/marketplace-checkout.html), and the router returns on first match — deleted, along with their 3 dispatch-table entries and their 2 stale references in the 404 hint list. Diagnosed 2026-07-08, left unfixed 3 days; removed now rather than left as silently-diverging duplicate implementations of a payment-adjacent path. The other 12 sub-actions (purchase, webhook, subscribe, subscriptions list, subscriptions/:id/cancel, /cancel alias, subscriptions/:id/upgrade, orders, entitlements, trial, roi-calculator, compare) were never shadowed and are unchanged by this fix. Of those, frontend/user-dashboard.html calls orders, entitlements, subscriptions (list), and cancel. No frontend caller found for purchase, webhook (server-to-server by design), subscribe, subscriptions/:id/upgrade, trial, roi-calculator, or compare — unchanged from the 2026-07-08 finding, NOT fixed in this pass (see notes for why this may be structural, not just unbuilt UI).

navigation.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html is a primary, well-linked page; the specific sub-routes it calls are reachable through it.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/marketplaceWebhook.test.mjs imports handleMarketplace directly (pre-existing). New workers/test/marketplaceDeadCodeRemoval.test.mjs (4 tests) exercises the REAL ROUTER (worker.fetch()) for GET /api/marketplace/catalog and /catalog/:productId, confirming they now return workers/src/handlers/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.js's real response shape (INR pricing, total_products — the removed dead code returned USD/total, so this also positively distinguishes which implementation answered); confirms a direct call to handleMarketplace() for the 3 removed paths now falls through to its own 404 rather than a shadowed handler; spot-checks 3 of the 12 untouched live sub-actions still work. This is the router-level test the 2026-07-08 finding flagged as missing (a direct-import-only test would not have caught the original shadowing bug).

verification.evidence: workers/src/index.js re-read to confirm current route-registration order and line numbers (both had shifted since 2026-07-08). workers/src/handlers/sentinelApexMarketplace.js's full internal dispatch re-read to confirm exactly which 3 sub-actions were shadowed vs. which 12 were not, and that PRODUCT_CATALOG (used by the 12 live ones) is distinct from and not shared with MARKETPLACE_CATALOG (workers/src/handlers/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.js, the catalog customers actually browse) — confirmed by diffing both catalogs' product-id sets programmatically: 12 ids in MARKETPLACE_CATALOG, 18 in PRODUCT_CATALOG, zero overlap. workers/test/marketplaceDeadCodeRemoval.test.mjs's 4 tests run against the real in-process router (worker.fetch()), not just handleMarketplace() in isolation. Full backend suite 210 files/2180 tests green (up from 209/2176 — the 1 new test file); node scripts/registry/validate.mjs 0 failures/0 warnings; scripts/seo-structure-lock.mjs 22/22 pages green. NOT YET verified against live deployed production.

notes: Two distinct findings, only the first fixed this pass. (1) FIXED: the shadowed catalog/product/checkout trio was dead code (CAP-MKT-002 is the real implementation for those 3 paths) — removed rather than left as a maintainer trap. (2) NOT FIXED, discovered while verifying (1), flagged for an explicit owner/product decision rather than guessed at: PRODUCT_CATALOG (18 products: api-free/pro/team/enterprise, kev-detection-pack, apt-yara-pack, soc-starter-kit, ai-spm-kit, apex-ultimate-bundle, etc. — used by this file's live purchase/subscribe/subscriptions-upgrade/compare/trial actions) shares ZERO product ids with MARKETPLACE_CATALOG (12 products: dp-ransomware-2025, dp-apt-north-korea, pb-ransomware-ir, cp-nist-csf-2, aa-threat-hunter, etc. — marketplaceCheckoutHandler.js, what a customer actually sees via GET /api/marketplace/catalog). A product_id a customer discovers by browsing the real, live catalog can never successfully resolve on subscribe/purchase/upgrade/compare/trial (each does `PRODUCT_CATALOG[product_id]`, which would be undefined for every id from the other catalog) — those 5 sub-actions are only reachable at all with a hardcoded PRODUCT_CATALOG-native id a caller already knows out-of-band. This may be exactly why the 2026-07-08 finding could not find any frontend caller for purchase/subscribe/subscriptions-upgrade/trial/roi-calculator/compare: wiring a UI to them from the browsable catalog would not have worked. Whether these represent two genuinely different product lines (e.g. one-time content downloads vs. recurring API/kit subscriptions) that were never meant to share an id space, or a real reconciliation is overdue, is a product decision this pass deliberately did not make — flagged per this registry's established convention for duplicate-system findings (see CAP-CRM-004/CAP-CRM-006) rather than guessing which catalog is canonical or merging them unilaterally.

name: Threat Intel Programmatic API (IOC/CVE/Actor/TTP/Risk)
description: A REST API surface for programmatic IOC enrichment, CVE detail, threat-actor lookup, TTP mapping, and risk scoring.

frontend.evidence: Zero matches anywhere in frontend/*.html for the exact paths /api/intel/ioc, /api/intel/cve, /api/intel/actor, /api/intel/threat-actors, or /api/intel/ttp. Frontend calls that look superficially related — frontend/intel-hub.html:550 (/api/intel/actors, plural) and frontend/threat-intel-workbench.html (/api/intel/risk-queue, /api/intel/risk-score/{id}, /api/intel/cve-brief/{id}, /api/intel/actors, /api/intel/actor/{id}) — are different paths registered to a different handler, workers/src/handlers/threatIntelPro.js, via a separate dispatch block (workers/src/index.js:5164-5186). Verified 2026-07-08 by reading both registration blocks directly.

navigation.evidence: No nav entry calls this handler's actual routes because no page does.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/intelRateLimitEnforcement.test.mjs imports handleIntelIOC directly from workers/src/handlers/intelAPIHandlers.js. Note: workers/test/honestCopy.test.mjs separately reads this same file as raw text (not a module import) to assert a pricing-flag string is present — a text-scan check, not a behavioral test, and not double-counted as coverage here.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/intelAPIHandlers.js exports enumerated; workers/src/index.js:5142-5186 read directly to confirm this handler's 5 routes and the separate workers/src/handlers/threatIntelPro.js dispatch block are genuinely distinct registrations, not aliases of each other.

notes: backend.status is "duplicate": a real, live, frontend-consumed threat-intel API surface already exists under near-identical naming in workers/src/handlers/threatIntelPro.js (covered in docs/capability-registry/domains/threat-hunting-intel.json) — this file appears to be an earlier or parallel implementation of the same concept that no frontend page was ever wired to. Worth a reconciliation decision rather than building new UI against this one.
